_readme: QA rubric consumed by tools/qa_image.py. Each defect code maps 1:1 to a guardrail
category in guardrails/ (tools/guardrails.py add --from-verdict uses the code).
severity 'fail' = asset must NOT feed a paid Kling job; 'warn' = usable but noted.
shot_types list which codes apply on top of 'universal' plus extra instructions.
QA checks DEFECTS only — creative taste stays human.

ANATOMY.check: Hands, fingers, limbs, teeth, eyes: wrong counts, merged/extra fingers, twisted joints, impossible limb positions, garbled teeth.

WARPED_TEXT.check: Any visible text (screen UI, signage, labels, clothing) that is warped, gibberish, misspelled, or pseudo-lettering.

DUPLICATED_OBJECTS.check: Duplicated or merged furniture/objects: two fused tables, a chair growing out of a sofa, doubled phones, cloned limbs or props.

SPATIAL_INTERSECTION.check: The character clips through, merges with, or is pinned between furniture/objects: body passing through a table edge, seated INSIDE a sofa arm, wedged unnaturally between two surfaces.

FLOATING_CHARACTER.check: The character is not physically grounded: hovering above the seat cushion or floor, feet not planted, sitting on air beside the chair, drifting posture with no support under them.

UNNATURAL_SEATING.check: The pose is physically implausible or looks odd/weird: seated at an impossible angle, torso disconnected from hips, leaning with no support, a pose no comfortable human would hold. The character must look naturally and comfortably seated or standing.

WATERMARK_ARTIFACT.check: Watermarks, logos of stock sites, residual grid lines, tiling seams, halo/JPEG artifacts.

IDENTITY_DRIFT.check: Compared to the PERSONA REFERENCE image: different face shape, age, skin tone, hairstyle, or facial features — must read as clearly the same person.

WARDROBE_DRIFT.check: Compared to the PERSONA REFERENCE (and any prior-shot context given): different garment, color, pattern, or accessories. Wardrobe must be consistent across all shots.

ENVIRONMENT_DRIFT.check: Compared to the stated environment/context: noticeably different room, lighting temperature, or background layout.

PHONE_BACK_TO_VIEWER.check: A held phone shows its back panel / camera bump to the viewer. FAIL unless the provided context explicitly says this is a reveal first-frame (back-to-viewer allowed only then).

WRONG_DEVICE.check: The phone is not the Black Samsung Galaxy S22 Ultra from the reference: iPhone notch/Dynamic Island, Apple logo, wrong color, wrong camera layout, rounded iPhone silhouette.

SCREEN_CONTENT_MISSING.check: For a screen-facing phone shot: the visible screen does NOT clearly show the expected app UI/logo (blank, generic, hallucinated UI, or illegible). Only applies when context says the screen should show the app.

PEOPLE_PRESENT.check: An environment plate that should contain no people has a person, partial figure, or reflection of one.

GIBBERISH_SIGNAGE.check: Background signage/posters with pseudo-text (acceptable if soft-focus and unreadable; warn if legible-but-gibberish).

GRID_ARTIFACT.check: Residual grid lines, neighboring-tile fragments, or collage borders in an extracted single portrait.

INCONSISTENT_TILES.check: In a 3x3 pose grid: identity, wardrobe, or lighting varies between tiles — every tile must show the same person, outfit, and light.

persona.instructions: A base-character portrait: front view, passport style. Judge realism and anatomy only; there is no reference to compare against.

environment.instructions: An empty environment plate. It must contain NO people and look like one coherent, physically plausible room/space.

grid.instructions: A 3x3 pose grid. Evaluate EVERY tile; report the tile position (row, column) in each defect's region. Identity/wardrobe must match the PERSONA REFERENCE in all nine tiles.

extract.instructions: A single extracted first-frame portrait that will seed a PAID video clip — this is the money gate, be strict. The character must be naturally grounded (seated or standing with real support), clearly the same person and wardrobe as the PERSONA REFERENCE.

phone-shot.instructions: A shot where the character holds the phone. DEFAULT RULE: the screen faces the viewer clearly showing the app UI/logo (compare against the APP UI REFERENCE if provided). Back-to-viewer is allowed ONLY if the context explicitly says this is a reveal first-frame.

broll-still.instructions: A B-roll start frame about to seed a paid muted clip. Judge against the given concept context; any phone in frame follows the screen-facing rule.

aroll-clip.instructions: First/last frames of a rendered A-roll clip. Verify the character stayed the same person with the same wardrobe and environment as the start frame / persona reference, still naturally grounded at both ends.

broll-clip.instructions: First/last frames of a rendered B-roll clip. Judge against the concept context; any phone screen shown must display the real app UI.
